description.s: Most places have two high tides per day - semi diurnal. Some places only have one high tide per day - diurnal - due to the shape of the coastline.
Time of high water gets later each day (by about 50 mins) due to lunar month (29.5 days) being shorter than calendar month.

Weather can also affect tide heights. Low pressure will increase height; high pressure will reduce it. High wind can also blow water towards/away from land, resulting in increase/decrease of tide height.
Flood Tide: going from low to high. Ebb Tide: going from high to low.
Tidal hour - measured as +/- N hours from HW. HW hour lasts +/- 30 mins from HW.
Tidal Range = High Water - Low Water. Can use it to compute Tidal Rate via Tidal Curve
